questions.question:
What does "developer conversation" mean in this README update: developer onboarding/communication, contributor discussion prompts, or developer conversion/adoption?

Why it matters: The phrase is ambiguous enough that the ticket could target different outcomes: clearer setup docs, better collaboration language, or a more persuasive README for attracting developers.
Recommended answer: Treat it as developer-facing communication: make the README clearer, more conversational, and more useful for contributors and coding agents without turning it into marketing copy.
questions.answer: Developer conversion is the ratio of developers that clone and try out the repo from github vs moving on to something else, the very top part of the readme must make them imediatly see the easy of setting up and how quickly they can use it, people dont like complicated setup, they like 2 commands max
